items.situation: Two students discuss a new restaurant downtown and which dish to order.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you try that new Thai place downtown? Everyone's talking about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went last week! Honestly, the green curry was good, but the mango sticky rice is what you really go for. It's incredible.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I usually skip dessert. Is it that special?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me. The rice is warm, the mango's super ripe. I'd go back just for that, even though it's a bit pricey.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. I'm meeting Lena there Friday, so I'll have to convince her to save room.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. Just don't fill up on the appetizers first.

questions.stem: Which dish does the man recommend most highly?
questions.explanation: 男生明确说芒果糯米饭才是真正值得去吃的（what you really go for），是他最推荐的菜。B绿咖喱他只说good，并非最推荐，属于wrong_detail。C开胃菜他反而提醒不要吃太多，属于misattributed。D红茶对话中根本没提到，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on Friday?

options.A: Cancel her dinner plans with Lena
options.B: Order only appetizers to save money
options.C: Try the dessert the man suggested
options.D: Eat at a different restaurant instead

questions.explanation: 女生原本不吃甜点，但被男生说服后表示要让Lena也留肚子（save room），暗示她打算尝尝那道甜点，所以C正确。A取消计划与对话相反，属于over_inference。B她反而被提醒别吃太多开胃菜，属于rejected_option。D换餐厅没有任何依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two roommates work out a fair cleaning schedule for shared spaces.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need a plan for the kitchen. It's getting out of hand, and I keep ending up cleaning it alone.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, sorry about that. My class schedule's been brutal. How about we just split it by week? I take this week, you take next?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, a whole week feels like too much at once. What if we each take specific days instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's smarter. Okay, I'll do Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays. You take the other days?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Deal. And let's put it on a sticky note on the fridge so nobody forgets.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll write it up tonight.

questions.stem: How do the roommates decide to divide the cleaning?

options.B: By assigning specific days to each person

questions.explanation: 两人最终决定按具体的星期几分工，男生负责周一三五，女生负责其余几天，所以B正确。A整周轮换是男生最初的提议，被女生否决了，属于rejected的wrong_detail。C请清洁服务对话从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D每晚一起打扫与他们分日子的方案相反，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.D: Write the schedule on a note for the fridge

questions.explanation: 男生说今晚会把分工写下来（I'll write it up tonight），而女生提议贴在冰箱上的便利贴，所以D正确。A今晚打扫厨房不是他答应的事，属于unrelated_next_step。B换室友过于极端，属于over_inference。C向宿管投诉对话毫无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two friends recommend a documentary series to each other.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You finished that nature documentary series, right? The one about deep oceans?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did, and honestly, I was skeptical at first. I figured it'd just be pretty footage with no real substance.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: And? Was it actually any good?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It surprised me. The science was solid, and each episode built on the last one. By the end I felt like I'd actually learned something.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow. I've got a long train ride home this weekend. Maybe I'll download it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Do it. Just start from episode one, though. They really build on each other.

questions.stem: What is the man mainly saying about the documentary series?

options.A: It has beautiful footage but little real content
options.B: It is too scientific for casual viewers
options.C: It was better and more informative than he expected
options.D: It would be good for a long train ride

questions.explanation: 男生主旨是这部纪录片超出他的预期，内容扎实、让他真正学到东西，所以C正确。A漂亮画面没内容正是他原本的怀疑，后来被推翻，属于reversed_situation。B太偏科学难懂对话没说，且与他的赞赏相反，属于wrong_topic。D适合长途火车是女生的想法，且只是一个细节，并非男生对纪录片的总体评价，属于too_narrow。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Watch the episodes in order from the beginning
options.B: Skip ahead to the most exciting episode
options.C: Watch it only after reading reviews
options.D: Find a shorter series for the train ride

questions.explanation: 男生强调要从第一集开始按顺序看，因为各集层层递进，所以A正确。B跳着看与他的建议相反，属于rejected_option。C看影评后再看对话没提，属于unrelated_next_step。D找更短的剧集没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two students compare how they get to campus each morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Do you take the bus to campus? I always see you near the stop.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, no. I just walk. It's only fifteen minutes from my place, and it wakes me up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow, lucky. I'm way too far to walk, so I bike. It's fast, but it's freezing this time of year.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yikes, biking in the cold sounds rough. Why not take the bus then?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's always packed in the morning, and it takes forever with all the stops.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair enough. Maybe just bundle up more until spring.

questions.stem: How does the man usually get to campus?
questions.explanation: 男生说他就是走路去（I just walk），只要十五分钟，所以D正确。A坐公交是女生看到他在车站附近的误会，他明确否认了，属于wrong_detail。B骑车是女生的方式，属于misattributed。C开车对话从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely keep doing to get to campus?

options.B: Keep biking but dress more warmly
options.D: Ask a friend for a ride each day

questions.explanation: 女生抱怨公交又挤又慢，男生建议她在春天前多穿点保暖，暗示她会继续骑车只是穿暖些，所以B正确。A改坐公交与她对公交的抱怨相反，属于rejected_option。C像男生一样走路她说自己住得太远走不了，属于rejected_option。D每天求人搭车对话毫无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two students plan what snacks and drinks to buy for a movie night.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for the movie night Saturday, what should we grab from the store?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely popcorn. You can't have a movie night without it. And maybe some soda?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good thinking. Although, half the group doesn't drink soda anymore. Maybe sparkling water instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, right. Let's do sparkling water then. And I'll grab some chocolate too.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll handle the popcorn and the drinks if you get the chocolate.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Deal. I'll stop by the store on my way home tomorrow.

questions.stem: What snack do both students agree is essential for the movie night?
questions.explanation: 男生说看电影一定要有爆米花（You can't have a movie night without it），女生也赞同，所以A正确。B巧克力只是男生额外加的，并非两人都认为必不可少，属于wrong_detail。C薯片和D饼干对话都没提到，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What drink will they most likely buy?
questions.explanation: 女生指出半数人不喝苏打水了，提议改买气泡水，男生也同意，所以C正确。A普通苏打水被女生否决了，属于rejected_option。B咖啡和D果汁对话都没提，属于unrelated_next_step。
